codeReview.summary: Permission tier system is correctly implemented in swell-core and swell-tools. The worker correctly identified that the feature was already implemented by a previous worker and verified it works as specified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' but this skill does not exist in .factory/skills/. The worker correctly noted this deviation and suggested updating feature skillName to use existing 'Rust' skill instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json specifies 'skillName': 'rust-worker' but ls .factory/skills/ shows only Rust, rust-patterns, rust-code-review, etc. No rust-worker exists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Commit message f6ed1d2 describes 'implement MCP client for external tool servers' but the handoff claims it implemented permission tiers. This appears to be a pre-existing feature that the worker verified rather than implemented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show f6ed1d2 diff shows changes to crates/swell-tools/src/mcp.rs, not permission tier code. The permission tier system already existed in swell-core/src/types.rs and tools.rs before this commit.

summary: The permission tier system (Auto/Ask/Deny permission tiers with Read/Write/Destructive risk levels per tool) is correctly implemented in the codebase. All 6 tools (ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ShellTool, GitTool, FileEditTool, SearchTool) have appropriate risk levels and permission tiers enforced by ToolExecutor with PermissionChecker. Build and tests pass. Worker correctly verified the feature was already complete and followed appropriate procedure given the missing rust-worker skill.
